notes: 32-byte IETF ChaCha20 key; byte-identical across every potassium sample we have analysed
12-byte IETF ChaCha20 nonce; sits at key+0x20 in every build
Single byte used for all C2 wire encoding (registration body and steady-state traffic in both directions)
Fixed 8-digit seed every potassium bot appends random digits to for its bot ID
TCP listener bound to discovered egress IP (127.0.0.1 fallback) as a single-instance lock
